deviations.description: The new requirement obligates doctor approval for discharge of patients over 65 or with more than 5 medications, but the process model only includes nurse approval for patients over 65 and does not address medication count or doctor approval. This results in a direct violation of the stricter requirement.
The process model does not require any approval (nurse or doctor) for patients with more than 5 medications, violating the new requirement that mandates doctor approval for such cases.

The process model enforces nurse approval for discharge of patients over 65, but the new requirement only requires doctor approval, making the nurse approval step unnecessary and more restrictive than required.
The process model enforces a 24-hour time constraint for nurse approval before discharge, but the new requirement does not specify any time constraint, resulting in unnecessary restriction.
